rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical epoxidation of alkenes using water as the oxygen source, catalyzed by a sterically hindered manganese porphyrin (TMP)MnCl under undivided-cell constant-current electrolysis.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of (TMP)Mn(III)(OH) to (TMP)Mn(IV)=O, which either disproportionates or is further oxidized at the anode to give the active [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+ oxidant; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: epoxide product via O-atom transfer to alkene

electron_flow: At the anode, (TMP)Mn(III)(OH) is oxidized by sequential single-electron transfers (via (TMP)Mn(IV)=O) to [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+, or two (TMP)Mn(IV)=O units disproportionate to give [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+ and (TMP)Mn(III)(OH); the Mn(V)=O then transfers its oxygen (derived from water) to the alkene to deliver the epoxide and regenerate Mn(III)(OH), while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: Generation of [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+ via single-electron oxidation/disproportionation of Mn(IV)=O at the anode

probe_reagents.name: H2 18-O isotope labeling (H2^18O as co-solvent)
probe_reagents.observation: MS of styrene oxide product 2a' showed ^18O incorporation, confirming that the epoxide oxygen originates from water rather than O2.

probe_reagents.name: N2 atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction performed under N2 excludes molecular oxygen as the O source.

probe_reagents.name: Constant-potential electrolysis at 0.75 V vs Fc+/Fc
probe_reagents.observation: At this potential Mn(III)-OH can be oxidized to Mn(IV)=O but Mn(IV)=O cannot be further oxidized; styrene still gave 2a in 87% GC yield (87% FE), supporting disproportionation of Mn(IV)=O to [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of (TMP)MnCl
probe_reagents.observation: Two redox peaks at 0.67 and 0.9 V vs Fc/Fc+ assigned to Mn(III)/Mn(IV) and Mn(IV)/Mn(V); the Mn(III)/Mn(IV) wave is reversible, indicating a stable Mn(IV) intermediate.

notes: Two pathways for forming [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+ are proposed and not fully distinguished: (a) two consecutive 1e- anodic oxidations of (TMP)Mn(III)(OH); (b) anodic oxidation to (TMP)Mn(IV)=O followed by disproportionation to [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+ + (TMP)Mn(III)(OH). The constant-potential experiment at 0.75 V (below the Mn(IV)/Mn(V) wave) supports the disproportionation pathway being operative. Contrast with prior salen-Mn electrochemistry, where SET gives (salen)Mn(IV)=O that does electrophilic chlorination/radical-type epoxidation; the bulky TMP ligand is invoked to suppress those off-pathway modes and favor Mn(V)=O formation. Pyridine (0.5 equiv) is required as an axial ligand/base.
